description: Removed Oracle database service and updated application configuration to support Azure Database for PostgreSQL. The oracle-db service has been completely removed, and environment variables have been added to support Managed Identity authentication to Azure PostgreSQL.
Updated Maven dependencies to replace Oracle JDBC driver with PostgreSQL driver and added Azure Identity Extensions for Managed Identity authentication support.

Migrated datasource configuration from Oracle to PostgreSQL Managed Identity authentication. Changed JDBC URL, driver class, and Hibernate dialect. Added comprehensive comments for Azure sovereign cloud configurations.
Updated Docker-specific properties to use PostgreSQL JDBC URL with Managed Identity authentication instead of Oracle connection string.
Updated H2 in-memory database configuration from Oracle compatibility mode to PostgreSQL compatibility mode for testing.

Migrated JPA entity mapping from Oracle to PostgreSQL. Removed @Lob annotation for Oracle BLOB storage, updated column definition types from Oracle NUMBER and TIMESTAMP DEFAULT SYSTIMESTAMP to PostgreSQL bigint and CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.

Migrated native SQL queries from Oracle syntax to PostgreSQL. Replaced ROWNUM-based pagination with LIMIT/OFFSET, replaced NVL with COALESCE, replaced TO_CHAR date functions with EXTRACT, and converted all table/column names to lowercase per PostgreSQL best practices. Window functions (RANK, SUM) remain compatible with both databases.
Updated comments and logging messages to reflect PostgreSQL bytea storage instead of Oracle BLOB storage. No functional changes to the business logic.
Updated comments and logging messages to reflect PostgreSQL bytea storage instead of Oracle BLOB storage. No functional changes to the API behavior or file serving logic.
